teaching_card.title: Polynomial Expressions (Medium)
teaching_card.body: To multiply two binomials, use FOIL: First, Outer, Inner, Last, then combine like terms. The coefficient of x comes from the Outer and Inner products added together.
key_points: The x² term comes from First; the constant from Last.

screens.title: Multiply with FOIL
screens.body: To expand (ax + b)(cx + d), multiply every pair: First, Outer, Inner, Last. The x² term is ac, the x term is (ad + bc), and the constant is bd. Combine the two middle (x) terms.
key_points: x coeff = a·d + b·c (add the two middle terms).

screens.title: Mind the signs
screens.body: Negative terms flip signs as you multiply. (x − 5)(x + 2) gives a constant of −10 and a middle term of −3x. Track each sign rather than rushing.
key_points: A negative times a positive is negative.
Recombine middle terms with their signs.

screens.title: Factoring is FOIL in reverse
screens.body: To factor x² + bx + c, find two numbers that multiply to c and add to b; those give the factors (x + p)(x + q). A factor (x − r) means r is a root that makes the trinomial zero.
key_points: Find two numbers: product c, sum b.

screens.title: Try the moves
screens.body: (x − 4)(x + 3) = x² − x − 12 (x² term 1, middle −x, constant −12). Reverse: x² − x − 12 factors back to (x − 4)(x + 3), so (x − 4) is a factor.
screens.worked_example: (x−4)(x+3) = x² − x − 12; so (x−4) is a factor of it.
